transcript:
Dmitry Fedotov: Shall we start with uh personal... personalities?

David Rutter: Yeah, I'll start with you if you don't mind. So, so we want to make this a little bit fun. I just met Dmitry. As you can tell, he's a buttoned-up regulator, and by the way, this is the first time I've worn a... worn a T-shirt to an event like this. But he's like, "Oh, I'm just a boring regulator." I'm like, "Oh really, what do you do for fun?" He goes, "Well, I fly helicopters, I fly planes, I'm a professional sky jumper, I was a Formula 3 racer, and uh, whatever the motorcycle thing is called." And I thought to myself, what a great way to start a conversation about why should you be considering Abu Dhabi when a regulator is not only forward-thinking but takes on that kind of personal risk and has that much fun and is that cool. So he's immediately became one of the coolest regulators I've ever met.

Dmitry Fedotov: Oh, thank you so much. So, uh, there is a twist to this. Um, it's all about risks, both in regulatory space and in my fun activities. You don't jump out of the plane without a plan and mapping out all the risks. Same as you don't go into uh registering a company or supervising a company uh without uh getting all your risks and all your ducks in a row. But uh, David, uh, you are not a boring person either. Uh, you're an American living in London. How does it feel?

David Rutter: Yes. That doesn't make me exciting, but I like to think I'm not a boring person. No.

Dmitry Fedotov: Uh, obviously, yeah. So, is... are there any cultural differences that you have to face every day? Um, does it reflect on performance of R3?

David Rutter: Well, well, and this kind of leads into our discussion, which is why be in Abu Dhabi. So, R3 got off to a uh, uh, a really hot start. We were very early into the blockchain space, focusing on real-world assets. Uh, and a lot of activity before there was regulatory clarity in the US was in Asia and here. And so, you know, we were early to move here about five or six years ago. And, you know, we've had a great experience. We've hired some great people. I've had an opportunity to learn about the culture. Um, clearly, there's... it's a great place to live. We've transferred a lot of people here and they're all extremely happy. It's a little hot in July and August. Um, but I think it's... it's a great place to do business. And I really think it was very prescient, and I'd like to get your... your view on this, that, you know, this... Abu Dhabi and ADGM, with the support of ADIO, which I think you should talk about for the young entrepreneurs, um, were pretty prescient in thinking about, okay, what do we need to succeed? We believe in the tokenized economy, the digital economy. And clearly, regulatory underpinnings is super important. And you were very early in that space. So maybe you can address that because I think having that regulatory certainty here is drawing a lot of businesses to the region.

Dmitry Fedotov: Um, indeed. Um, so we have uh quite a few principles in our approaches. Number one is we are industry agnostic. So if you are in staking, lending, uh, let's say borrowing, uh, we will assess the business same as in TradFi. If the... if there is a analogy in TradFi, we apply same principles. Obviously, there will be some uh... uh... unique points like uh custody and management of keys, but general principles apply in the same way. Um, number two, uh, I guess uh... it's uh... it's worth mentioning the history. So for those of you who have been in the industry for... for... for a while, um, you may remember this great migration. So a lot of exchanges, for example, started off uh in China, migrated to Hong Kong, then to Singapore, then Malta, then, you know, Europe or... or... wherever. And uh, we in ADGM, we were the first jurisdiction to come up with comprehensive virtual asset regulations uh back in 2018. Um, so uh, for those of you who remember 2018, it was a bloodbath obviously. So this lady from OneCoin uh... uh... I don't remember her name at the moment, disappeared. BitConnect collapsed in January. Uh, all ICOs were dying left and right. Yeah. But we took this proactive approach and we created regulatory certainty, uh, and we have stuck to this uh... um... ideology up until now without diverging. We have not changed our... change... changed our position. And that helps a lot because uh... because um, well, one of the benefits of UAE is political certainty and political stability. We have no four or eight year cycle where you have uh different leaders uh having different opinions on uh... on... on uh crypto. But uh, my question to you, um, uh, would be, um, what is your experience? So like, uh, you have been here for a while. Yes. You... you had experience with us as regulators, you had experience with sovereign wealth funds. Uh, obviously it's one of our... our major... let's call it sales points, right? You have access to capital, you have access to the uh... reg... regulatory cer... certainty, infrastructure. Uh, what's your take?

David Rutter: Well, I, you know, we've talked about having that foundation is really key. And while I've seen so much growth here the last five years, I'm really excited about the future for the following reasons. You know, we have... we talk about how we have 17 billion uh on our... on our platform. Exactly. That... that's a pittance, guys. It's really about the 17 trillion, the 35 trillion, how we tokenize the real-world economy. And if you look at this region, it's... first off, geographically, the location is... is ideal. There's a ton of capital here. They talk about how they are the capital of capital. But it's smart money. And that money is deployed in industries across the globe. Okay? So if we look at the international investments that originate from this place, and you believe in a tokenized economy, we're working on some really cool stuff with shipbuilders. A lot of the ship... ships are 100 to 300 million dollars to build. They're funded by sovereign wealth funds. We look at real estate, gold tokenization, and other hard assets that are being tokenized. And I assure you that uh the sovereign wealth funds and investment is coming from this region. Then if you think about just the growth here, and... and ADGM continues to expand, we were talking about that earlier, you know, in... I look at... at the tokenized economy as, let's say I... I believe in Abu Dhabi real estate. You know, I can invest in an equity of a company that builds here. That's fine, but it's... it's rather imperfect. If I'm wealthy enough and I'm part of a private equity uh and have a family office, I can take part in a loan syn... syndication specific to a building. But they're already tokenizing land titles. So in the future, I can have... I can create a source of new capital for... for those that are building uh these amazing buildings, but I can also benefit in the returns, which I think is amazing. And that's going to happen here. One of the reasons it's going to happen here is these capital markets are developed, but not as developed as they are elsewhere. And there's an open-mindedness that I think is going to drive the token economy, and a lot of it's going to be done right here.

Dmitry Fedotov: Amazing. Amazing. Uh, good to hear. Um, what's on your roadmap for the next 12 months in the region?

David Rutter: So, so in the region, first off, you know, uh, Abu Dhabi is an amazing place. Um, but the entire Gulf has uh a number of opportunities. You know, one thing about doing business here is you really have to invest the time. The relationships still... still matter. And for someone of... of my age, that's important, you know. Um, and... and you have to... to really invest the time, think about your roadmap. For us, R3 has gone through this important transition where we worked closely with banks and CSDs and other uh TradFi institutions that move rather slowly. We had this relationship that we announced with Solana in May, and we are now all in on driving assets into the Solana ecosystem. But in a way that makes sense for on-chain investors. And that mismatch occurs today where people think, okay, I'm just going to put some yielding assets onto... on-chain and everyone's going to eat it up. That's not true. We're really focusing on understanding the drivers from the on-chain investors, understanding how Wall Street f... you know, chases the money, how they can build a product that is available and interesting uh to folks on-chain. So that's what we're going to focus on. We're going to continue to work closely with sovereign funds and industries because we believe in the tokenized economy goes way past money, goes way past bonds, you know, it's all... all of real-world assets and so much of that uh exists in this region.

Dmitry Fedotov: Amazing. So uh quite a few announcements were made in recent days uh during Abu Dhabi Finance Week. Uh, one of the biggest is uh Binance migrated to ADGM. And by... by that I mean Binance Global that was kind of uh domiciled in Caymans for... for a while but not really regulated anywhere. So 300 million users are now uh... uh... regulated out of ADGM. Um, I literally just came from a ceremony where we signed with uh Tether uh... um... on enhancing the programs uh that enable uh transactions in virtual assets. So we essentially enabled, as... as of now already, uh... uh... retail, B2B, B2G uh transactions in uh more or less uh all top 100 by coin market cap uh assets uh in our jurisdiction. So you can buy properties with... with virtual assets, you can pay your taxes, uh do whatever you want. Uh, and this is really the focus uh of Tether. So uh... uh... and this and... and... and other announcements, will this enable you to reach a trillion in tokenized assets? And when?

David Rutter: Yeah, I... I... like I said, I think it's the foundation upon which you... you build. Um, and... and that... that clarity really helps. We still have some wood to chop. But uh, you know, I hate making predictions, but it's not going to be that long. And also, as many speakers addressed today, it's kind of exponential growth, right? We're still... the... the money market funds and everything else that are tokenized is still really, really small. But we will normalize that. It's one of the things we're focusing on, making it super easy uh to come online. And um, you know, just doing another pitch for doing business here. I can tell you, so I was the CEO of BrokerTec and EBS, a couple regulated exchanges, and early in my career, engagements with the regulators were... were quite friendly and open-minded. And then we went through this period where you'd get a briefing every time uh before you go to talk to someone. I think one of the things here is that they're really open-minded to new ideas. They're really try... they're willing to... to... to try new things. Uh, and so I think that's going to help us and anyone else who's considering doing... doing business here. You know, importantly, there is competition now. Like, you know, the United States is... is evolving rapidly with their... well, not as rapidly as we'd like, with their regulatory regime. Uh, but I think the perfect balance is... is... is the US and New York and... and seeing Abu Dhabi as the two centers that really drive the token economy uh over the next decade or so.

Dmitry Fedotov: Amazing. Amazing answer. And uh just closing remark for myself. I see we are a bit over time already. Um, for those of you who are considering establishing your presence here, whether you are in uh crypto or blockchain related business or you are playing with AI, uh any sort of emerging technologies, look me up. I'm very proactive on LinkedIn. Um, uh, and... and feel free to drop me a line. I'm... I'm really very accessible. Uh, David, thank you so much.

David Rutter: Thank you guys. Let's go Solana.

Dmitry Fedotov: Thanks to Solana and uh have a great event ahead. Thank you.

David Rutter: Cheers.

sources.title: Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana

Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium

# ![Logo_Purple_w bg.png](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/33b684d4-4115-45ef-849e-b207935b0b70?size=3)
# Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana
December 04, 2025 09:00 ET| Source:[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/FT%20Intermediate%CE%B4%20Inc%C2%A7)FollowFigure Technology Solutions, Inc.
Share
* * * * * * * * ![Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/38b97e43-d99f-4a08-9e38-3577453d7f24/rwa-consortium.png)[](#)
*New coalition advances Figure’s vision of moving capital markets to blockchain, expands real-world yield to Solana, and sets the standard for RWA collaboration across chains*
SAN FRANCISCO, Dec. 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=sVrR0nCGbyoSGqLpmp3W226XOcDwCfx8V3EteYCwLGxXEo0igM8ivJlwMreqY9enDApKlqpkq2h9LGXlpppDneFNwYs_wf55flepAb6tunwEmh-xksGmM2ldyKQcRF4H)(“Figure”)(Nasdaq: FIGR), the leading blockchain-native capital marketplace for the origination, funding, sale, and trading of on-chain loan products and tokenized real-world assets (RWAs), today announced the launch of an RWA consortium, a landmark coalition that will expand access to Figure's more than $1 billion in monthly on-chain loan originations, starting on Solana. The consortium represents a coordinated effort by leading crypto platforms to support and drive adoption of PRIME, the liquid staking token built on the[Hastra liquidity protocol](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=4qB14oW0HW3wV6G7eIeftmMVA8az3rMOK8PAa0Y2-BRXRv-AIPxXC8jou5QwqZswqZL167DOJEXxgNXklTx7X5zNuk4nN-6TXUv6pOrCjxitPRgZlNKhBddz_5Xu1T9Hu1XH4kRiKqkttkiJeicO8MN-CNu8gmEyyquNATcxaPNC56uSFgFkmxuV7i1s-QvGo4s2-XxOi1gshATrL0nmDL5c78zLpeU5MWvBvHd1TKM=), developed in partnership with Figure and the Provenance Blockchain Foundation. PRIME is powered by the Democratized Prime decentralized lending protocol on the Provenance Blockchain, which allows users to lend against pools of tokenized Figure RWA loans. Through this coalition, everyday DeFi users can now access institutional-grade yields previously reserved for banks and accredited investors.
The RWA Consortium builds on Figure’s vision to bring capital markets on-chain, a goal most recently reinforced during its IPO and the expansion of its RWA ecosystem across multiple blockchains. By extending beyond the Provenance Blockchain to integrate with Layer 1 networks like Solana, Figure is partnering with Hastra to leverage Chainlink[CCIP](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=AoAEgzHaPbSD8H81yfVKVDXA32VlG1HlMnUL10_xbh1thIeREQF8WzIHuEqW8sPg7s78VxMYQfWySLEd-xz4Tg==)to power the cross-chain interoperability needed for a connected, multi-chain finance ecosystem. With over $19 billion in on-chain loan originations to date and a[70% market share](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=_8YSDjYvrN99A8tUsqQH01Y3JxsV6a895BAreWpCDDEb82mue5e6XzzI_TsT7oYru-CPY3g5Xe0uEA769WrIxfR1KhHLCDiIcXYiXdT8nwu4hb9Amh8nUkhSFupuvO2d)in the RWA private credit, Figure is uniquely positioned to bridge traditional credit markets with DeFi.
This initiative follows the recent debut of Hastra, a liquidity protocol incubated by Figure and Provenance Blockchain that allows the yields generated in Democratized Prime to reach decentralized finance, with all Hastra yield products secured by the Chainlink data standard. Hastra acts as a

sources.title: Plume Brings Institutional Real-World Yield to Solana With Launch of RWA Nest Vaults
sources.snippet:
Plume Brings Institutional Real-World Yield to Solana With Launch of RWA Nest Vaults[Accessibility Statement](https://www.cision.com/about/accessibility/)[Skip Navigation](#main)
# Plume Brings Institutional Real-World Yield to Solana With Launch of RWA Nest Vaults
[![Plume Network Logo (PRNewsfoto/Plume Network)]( "Plume Network Logo (PRNewsfoto/Plume Network)")](#)
## News provided by
[**Plume Network**](https://www.prnewswire.com/news/plume-network/)
Dec 05, 2025, 09:15 ET

*Nest vaults with assets from WisdomTree, Hamilton Lane, BlackOpal and issuers including Securitize and SuperState mark the beginning of a new yield economy forSolana.*
NEW YORK,Dec. 5, 2025/PRNewswire/ --[Plume](https://edge.prnewswire.com/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4573545-1&h=1349188964&u=https://plume.org/&a=Plume), the leading RWA blockchain powering real-world yield, today announced the launch of five RWA Nest vaults onSolana, setting real-world yield as a new base layer for the ecosystem and marking an important first step in Plume's multichain expansion for Nest.
Launching the vaults natively onSolanagives users direct investment opportunities to institutional-grade assets from WisdomTree, Hamilton Lane, BlackOpal, and issuers including Securitize and SuperState.Solanausers can natively access onchain credit, treasuries, and short-term receivables and the ability to earn institutional-grade real-world yield.
With the launch of nBASIS, nOPAL, nWISDOM, nALPHA and nTBILL Vaults,Solana's 20+ million users can soon deposit stablecoins natively into Nest to unlock reliable, stable, real-world APY. Each deposit returns a Nest vaulttoken, a liquid, yield-bearing asset that accrues value over time and can move seamlessly acrossSolanaDeFi, from DEX liquidity pools to lending and structured products, with the ability to redeem at any point.
As part of this integration, Plume is also launching a deep partnership with[Squads Lab](https://edge.prnewswire.com/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4573545-1&h=2271106279&u=https://squads.xyz/&a=Squads+Lab),Solana's leading multi-sig and treasury management infrastructure provider. Plume will introduce its RWA yield pipelines across the Squads ecosystem, including a bespoke vault integrated for Altitude users and seamless RWA yield access for developers building with Grid.
*"Cryptois moving past the era of synthetic yield and into a market grounded in real economics. Stablecoins brought millions intocrypto, but yieldcoins will keep them here. This integration withSolanais the next step in building a transparent, programmable financial system at scale."*said Teddy Pornprinya, Co-Founder and CBO at Plume.
*"Plume already anchors over half of the industry's RWA activity, andSolanahas become one of the most efficient, high-throughput environments incrypto, making it a natural home for onchain credit, treasuries, and short-term receivables. Bringing our strengths together is how we unlock the next phase of onchain finan*ce".
By introducing institutional RWAs at scale, Plume is expanding theSolanaDeFiecosystem with sustainable, composable yield infrastructure that can underpin the next generation ofSolana-native financial applications.
Solanausers can instantly access boosted yield opportunities with Nest.
The Plume Nest Points Program is a rewards framework designed to turn participation into ownership. Users can earn Plume Nest Points by holding and using Nest vault tokens. The more users hold and deploy, the more they earn as part of the Season One program.
Solanausers will also soon unlock boosted yield opportunities with direct access to lending markets on Loopscale. These platform integrations allow leverage

tags.impact_reason: Discussion of Abu Dhabi's regulatory environment attracting RWA businesses to the region, indicating a solid development for Solana's ecosystem, especially given related news headlines.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a skydiver as the Semantic Anchor, directly inspired by the transcript where the ADGM regulator describes his passion for sky jumping. This visually represents the concept of 'calculated risk'—a theme he explicitly connects to financial regulation. The skydiver descending towards a futuristic Abu Dhabi symbolizes major entities like Binance and R3 making a precise, planned entry into this new, forward-thinking jurisdiction. I included the diegetic text 'RWA' as a holographic HUD on the skydiver's visor to anchor the high-concept visual to the story's core topic: bringing Real-World Assets to the blockchain.
visual_idea.description: A high-angle shot following a figure in a sleek, aerodynamic wingsuit made of matte carbon fiber, descending with extreme precision towards a glowing, futuristic Abu Dhabi skyline at dusk. The city is a dense grid of geometric towers and light trails. Inside the figure's helmet, a faint, glowing holographic Heads-Up Display (HUD) is projected onto the visor, displaying the diegetic text 'RWA'. The atmosphere is one of intense focus and calculated, high-stakes action against a backdrop of immense architectural scale.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A high-angle shot following a figure in a sleek, aerodynamic wingsuit made of matte carbon fiber, descending with extreme precision towards a glowing, futuristic Abu Dhabi skyline at dusk. The city below is a dense grid of geometric towers and intricate light trails, bathed in soft neon mixed lighting and casting soft blended neon shadows. Inside the figure's helmet, a faint, glowing holographic Heads-Up Display (HUD) is projected onto the visor, displaying the diegetic text 'RWA', reminiscent of racing helmet displays and holographic elements. The atmosphere is one of intense focus and calculated, high-stakes action against a backdrop of immense architectural scale. Composed for a vertical aspect ratio (3:4), emphasizing immense height and epic scale, with significant negative space above the figure to enhance the monumental descent. High-contrast cyber-noir atmosphere, reflective surfaces, volumetric lighting, photorealistic. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, cinematic lighting, cyberpunk atmosphere.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a skydiver as the Semantic Anchor, directly inspired by the transcript where the ADGM regulator describes his passion for sky jumping. This visually represents the concept of 'calculated risk'—a theme he explicitly connects to financial regulation. The skydiver descending towards a futuristic Abu Dhabi symbolizes major entities like Binance and R3 making a precise, planned entry into this new, forward-thinking jurisdiction. I included the diegetic text 'RWA' as a holographic HUD on the skydiver's visor to anchor the high-concept visual to the story's core topic: bringing Real-World Assets to the blockchain.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on the side of the skydiver's high-tech helmet. The polished, dark visor reflects a distorted, fish-eye view of the sprawling neon city grid below. Sharp, specular highlights trace the aerodynamic curves of the helmet, revealing the intricate weave of the carbon fiber material and emphasizing the theme of advanced technology and precision engineering.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A perfectly symmetrical, centrally focused, tight macro shot of the side of a skydiver's high-tech helmet. The polished, dark visor reflects a distorted, fish-eye view of a sprawling neon city grid below, with holographic elements and glowing data projections subtly visible within the urban landscape. Sharp, dramatic specular highlights trace the aerodynamic curves of the helmet, intensely revealing the intricate weave of the carbon fiber material and emphasizing the theme of advanced technology and precision engineering. The helmet surface is hyper-detailed with fine textures, catching the soft mixed neon lighting – pink from the left and blue from the right – casting subtle, blended neon shadows. The composition fills the square frame (1:1 aspect ratio) with rich detail and reflective surfaces, creating a 'Neon-Drenched' and 'Holographic Interface' aesthetic. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, volumetric light, ultra-sharp focus, studio quality, dark contrast.

editorial.headline: ADGM Onboards Binance Global as R3 Pivots to Solana
editorial.standfirst: Abu Dhabi Global Market cements its status as a crypto superpower by regulating Binance’s 300-million-user global entity and enabling tax payments via Tether. Simultaneously, institutional heavyweight R3 commits to driving sovereign-backed real-world assets onto Solana.
key_takeaways: Binance Global has migrated its domicile to ADGM, bringing 300 million users under Abu Dhabi regulation.
ADGM signed a partnership with Tether to allow government tax and property payments using top 100 cryptocurrencies.
R3 is pivoting to Solana to tokenize heavy industrial assets, including $100M+ commercial ships funded by sovereign wealth.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative that regulation stifles innovation has been effectively dismantled in Abu Dhabi. During a fireside chat at Solana Breakpoint, **ADGM** regulator Dmitry Fedotov disclosed that **Binance Global** has officially migrated its domicile to the jurisdiction, bringing its massive 300-million-user base under ADGM oversight. This move, coupled with a newly signed agreement with **Tether** to enable government and B2B payments in major cryptocurrencies, positions Abu Dhabi not merely as a regional hub, but as the primary regulatory clearing layer for the institutional digital economy.

For investors, the signal is clear: smart capital is moving on-chain, and it is originating from the Gulf. **R3** CEO David Rutter, whose firm has historically focused on private banking consortiums, declared the company is now "all in" on **Solana**. Rutter highlighted a critical mismatch between current on-chain yields and Wall Street's requirements, noting that R3’s pivot is designed to bridge this gap by bringing high-quality, sovereign-backed assets to the public chain.

The scale of these assets dwarfs current DeFi TVL. Rutter pointed to active initiatives with shipbuilders to tokenize vessels costing between $100 million and $300 million. This represents the transition from the "17 billion" currently on R3's platform to the "17 trillion" dollar real-world economy opportunity. With ADGM providing the legal rails for these tokenized assets to be treated with the same certainty as traditional financial instruments, the infrastructure is now in place for a massive repricing of industrial assets on Solana.
